<?php
/**
 * cms/api/translate-all.php
 * POST: Re-translates every cms_content row from DE → EN using the
 *       currently configured TranslationService (DeepL / LibreTranslate).
 *
 * Only ADMIN users may trigger this. Silent fail per-row so a single bad
 * field never aborts the whole batch.
 *
 * Response: { success, total, translated, errors, last_error? }
 */
declare(strict_types=1);
define('BASE_PATH', dirname(__DIR__));
require_once BASE_PATH . '/config/bootstrap.php';
require_once BASE_PATH . '/includes/cms-content.php';

// Long-running task — bump timeout
@set_time_limit(600);
@ini_set('memory_limit', '256M');

cmsEnsureSchema();

$user = requireAdmin(jsonMode: true);

// CSRF: accept header OR body token
$body  = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input') ?: '{}', true) ?? [];
$token = $_SERVER['HTTP_X_CSRF_TOKEN'] ?? $body['csrf_token'] ?? '';
if (!empty($_SESSION['csrf_token']) && !hash_equals($_SESSION['csrf_token'], $token)) {
    if (empty($_SESSION['user_id'])) {
        jsonError('Nicht authentifiziert.', 401);
    }
}

use Maxiworx\CMS\SettingsRepository;
use Maxiworx\Translation\TranslationService;

$db       = getDbConnection();
$settings = new SettingsRepository($db);

$apiKey = trim((string)$settings->get('translation_api_key', ''));
if ($apiKey === '') {
    jsonError('Kein Translation-API-Key konfiguriert.', 422);
}

try {
    $translator = new TranslationService($db, $settings);
} catch (\Throwable $e) {
    jsonError('TranslationService nicht verfügbar: ' . $e->getMessage(), 500);
}

// Pull rows where EN may be (re)translated. Two cases qualify:
//   1. en_manual = 0 (auto-eligible, normal case)
//   2. STALE: TRIM(value_en) = TRIM(value_de)  — EN is a literal copy of DE,
//      which can never be a real manual translation. Older saves with broken
//      DeepL leave these in the DB, sometimes incorrectly flagged en_manual=1.
//      We override the flag for these.
//
// Rows that are en_manual = 1 AND have a different EN text are PRESERVED.
try {
    $rows = $db->query(
        "SELECT id, value_de FROM cms_content
         WHERE TRIM(value_de) <> ''
           AND (en_manual = 0 OR TRIM(value_en) = TRIM(value_de))"
    )->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
} catch (\Throwable $e) {
    // Older schema without en_manual column → fall back to all rows
    $rows = $db->query(
        "SELECT id, value_de FROM cms_content WHERE TRIM(value_de) <> ''"
    )->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
}

// Count how many rows are genuinely manual (different EN text) and were skipped
$preserved = 0;
try {
    $preserved = (int)$db->query(
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cms_content
         WHERE TRIM(value_de) <> ''
           AND en_manual = 1
           AND TRIM(value_en) <> TRIM(value_de)"
    )->fetchColumn();
} catch (\Throwable $e) { /* swallow */ }

$total      = count($rows);
$translated = 0;
$errors     = 0;
$lastError  = null;

// Try the update with en_manual reset; fall back if column missing
$updateWithFlag = null;
$updatePlain    = $db->prepare(
    'UPDATE cms_content SET value_en = ?, updated_by = ? WHERE id = ?'
);
try {
    $updateWithFlag = $db->prepare(
        'UPDATE cms_content SET value_en = ?, updated_by = ?, en_manual = 0 WHERE id = ?'
    );
} catch (\Throwable $e) { /* older schema */ }

foreach ($rows as $row) {
    try {
        $en = $translator->translate((string)$row['value_de'], 'de', 'en');
        if (trim($en) === '') {
            $errors++;
            continue;
        }
        if ($updateWithFlag !== null) {
            $updateWithFlag->execute([$en, (int)$user['id'], (int)$row['id']]);
        } else {
            $updatePlain->execute([$en, (int)$user['id'], (int)$row['id']]);
        }
        $translated++;
    } catch (\Throwable $e) {
        $errors++;
        $lastError = $e->getMessage();
    }
}

jsonResponse([
    'success'    => true,
    'total'      => $total,
    'translated' => $translated,
    'preserved'  => $preserved,
    'errors'     => $errors,
    'last_error' => $lastError,
]);

<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

/**
 * cms/project.php — Project-specific configuration.
 *
 * THIS is the file that gets rewritten for each new site built on top of
 * the CMS. Everything project-specific lives here:
 *
 *   - Identity (name, tagline, footer line, supported languages)
 *   - Branding (accent colour, fonts) — full token system arrives in Phase 2
 *   - Visual-editor page list
 *   - Per-field character limits (supports wildcards: `artist-*.name`)
 *   - Per-page hero / section default content (auto-seeded into the DB)
 *   - Translation-protected terms (brand names, technical jargon)
 *   - Active feature flags
 *   - Custom modules
 *   - Minimum required CMS-core version
 *
 * The CMS core (cms/src/, cms/api/, cms/includes/, …) is generic and MUST
 * NOT contain Maxiworx-specific values. If you ever find yourself hard-
 * coding a brand string or content key inside core, lift it here and read
 * via the cmsProject() / cmsBrand() / cmsPages() / … helpers in
 * cms/includes/cms-content.php.
 *
 * SECURITY: This file is a pure config consumed via `require`. The guard
 * below returns 404 on direct URL access so the configuration cannot be
 * fetched as raw output if the web server were ever misconfigured to
 * serve PHP as plain text.
 *
 * @return array<string,mixed>
 */

// ── Block direct web access ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
if (!empty($_SERVER['SCRIPT_FILENAME'])
    && realpath($_SERVER['SCRIPT_FILENAME']) === realpath(__FILE__)) {
    http_response_code(404);
    exit;
}

Tipp:

``` The text after `` is a raw text node — no wrapping element with `data-cms-key`. So: - `applyCmsContent()` in i18n.js can't find it to swap it - Even if the DB has `step2.tip` with a German `value_de`, translate-all would create `value_en`, but without `data-cms-key` the swap never happens client-side - The user says even "Alle Inhalte übersetzen" doesn't help, which means `step2.tip` likely doesn't exist in the DB at all — the fallback text is always showing because `c()` returns empty, so translate-all has nothing to translate 2. **Step 3 Privacy**: In `booking.php` line 773, there's a `data-cms-key="step3.privacy"` wrapping a raw HTML output... The `renderRichText()` function in i18n.js only allows `
`, ``, ``, ``, `` tags — not `` tags. So when the translation swaps in, it strips out the link. Plus, if `step3.privacy` isn't in the DB at all, translate-all won't create it, and even if it did, the `` tag would get stripped anyway. For `step2.tip`, the issue is there's no `data-cms-key` on the text node, so i18n.js can't swap it, and the DB row probably doesn't exist either. The fix is to move this to `data-i18n` instead — add `booking.tip` to both DE and EN in i18n.js and have the PHP output a static `` without DB dependency. For `step3.privacy`, the link complicates things since `renderRichText()` strips `` tags. I could split the text around the link — wrapping the parts before and after in separate `data-i18n` spans while keeping the anchor tag intact. But a cleaner approach is to use `data-de` and `data-en` attributes directly on the span containing the full HTML, then modify `applyDualLangText()` to set `innerHTML` instead of `textContent`. Though that introduces a security risk if the content isn't properly sanitized. For the privacy text specifically, since the link to datenschutz.php is constructed server-side and doesn't change between languages, I could split the text around the link — putting the pre- and post-link text into separate i18n keys while keeping the HTML link itself intact. This way the surrounding text gets translated through the i18n system while the link remains as static HTML. I can see the approach now—breaking the privacy text into separate translatable spans with the link isolated in the middle keeps it clean and avoids any innerHTML security issues. For the tip section, I'll move it to a pure i18n string in the translations file rather than pulling from the database. Then I need to update the booking.php template to use the new span structure with data-i18n attributes, replacing the PHP echo logic with the safer markup approach. I'm refactoring the HTML to use i18n attributes for internationalization, breaking up the privacy checkbox label into separate translatable spans so each part can be independently localized, and updating the tip section to use the same i18n pattern. Adding the English privacy suffix... I'm settling on this approach because it avoids database dependencies, doesn't require translating everything at once, and the live swap happens instantly without any security concerns or unnecessary complexity.

Translate-all hat keinen Row zum übersetzen. **step3.privacy**: Hat `data-cms-key`, aber `renderRichText()` in i18n.js whitelistet nur `
////` — **`` wird rausgestripped**. Außerdem vermutlich auch kein DB-Row. **Fix**: Beide Texte als `data-i18n` UI-Strings (kein DB nötig, kein Translate-all nötig). Privacy-Link wird in drei Teile gesplittet so dass `` als reines HTML-Element stehen bleibt.
